description:
Write and execute a PySAM Python script that models a 7 kW residential PV system in Phoenix, AZ, under a 3-tier increasing block utility rate structure with net metering. The simulation should combine the Pvwattsv8, Utilityrate5, and Cashloan modules to generate a financial summary. Save the script to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/bill_savings_analysis.py and the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/phoenix_bill_savings.json.

System Parameters:
- Location: Phoenix, AZ (use a TMY weather file from SAM's solar_resource directory; check /home/ga/.SAM/solar_resource_dir.txt)
- DC system capacity: 7.0 kW
- Module type: 0 (Standard)
- Array type: 1 (Fixed - roof mount)
- Tilt: 20 degrees
- Azimuth: 180 degrees
- DC-to-AC ratio: 1.2
- System losses: 14.08%
- Inverter efficiency: 96%

Monthly Household Electricity Consumption (kWh) from Jan to Dec:
[750, 700, 800, 900, 1100, 1400, 1600, 1500, 1300, 1000, 800, 750]

Tiered Utility Rate Structure:
- Monthly fixed/service charge: $12.50/month
- Tier 1: First 400 kWh/month at $0.0925/kWh
- Tier 2: Next 400 kWh/month (401-800 kWh) at $0.1115/kWh
- Tier 3: All usage above 800 kWh/month at $0.1378/kWh
- Net metering: Enabled
- No demand charges

Financial Parameters:
- Total installed cost: $3.00/Wdc ($21,000 total)
- Federal Investment Tax Credit (ITC): 30%
- Analysis period: 25 years
- Nominal discount rate: 5.5%
- Annual module degradation: 0.5%/year
- Annual electricity rate escalation: 2.5%/year
- Loan: Assume cash purchase (no loan)
- State/local incentives: None

The JSON results file must contain exactly these keys:
{
  "annual_energy_kwh": <number>,
  "annual_bill_without_solar_dollars": <number>,
  "annual_bill_with_solar_dollars": <number>,
  "first_year_savings_dollars": <number>,
  "npv_25yr_dollars": <number>,
  "simple_payback_years": <number>
}
